feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Strategy
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Strategy Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP Basis Administration
entry.content:
Comprehensive Guide to the Key Responsibilities of a SAP Basis Administrator

Embarking on a career as a SAP Basis administrator opens up a world of responsibilities that are pivotal to the functioning of SAP systems.

This guide delves deep into the core tasks that define this role, providing beginners with a roadmap to navigate their way through the intricacies of SAP Basis administration.

Installation and Configuration: 

The first and foremost responsibility of a SAP Basis administrator is to install and configure SAP systems. This involves setting up servers, databases, and other components, and configuring them to work harmoniously. Installation and configuration are the building blocks upon which the entire SAP environment rests.

User Administration:

User administration is a critical aspect of SAP Basis administration. Basis administrators are tasked with creating and managing user accounts, assigning roles and authorizations, and ensuring that users have the necessary access to SAP functions based on their roles within the organization. Effective user administration is essential for maintaining system security and ensuring smooth operations.

System Monitoring:

System monitoring is an ongoing task that involves keeping a vigilant eye on the performance and health of SAP systems. This includes monitoring system resources such as memory, CPU usage, and disk space, as well as monitoring for any potential issues or errors that may arise. System monitoring is crucial for ensuring optimal performance and identifying and addressing issues before they escalate.

Transport Management:

Transport management is a key responsibility of a SAP Basis administrator. This involves managing the transport of changes, such as configuration settings or program developments, from one SAP system to another. The Transport Management System (TMS) is used to ensure that changes are moved in a controlled and consistent manner, maintaining system consistency and integrity.

Backup and Recovery:

Backup and recovery are indispensable components of SAP Basis administration. Basis administrators must regularly back up SAP systems to safeguard against data loss. In the event of a system failure, they must be able to swiftly recover the system and restore it to a working state. Backup and recovery procedures are essential for ensuring system availability and data integrity.

Being a SAP Basis administrator entails a multifaceted role that demands a combination of technical prowess, attention to detail, and proactive management.

By mastering these key responsibilities, you can navigate the complexities of SAP Basis administration and contribute significantly to the efficiency and stability of SAP systems.

Red Hat Enterprise Linux #SAP Basis Basis Technology 

Digital Technologies SAP TechEd High Tech SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Adaptive Server Enterprise

entry.title: Budget saving in Purchase Requests (PRs)
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Firstly, I would like to extend my apologies if my question reveals a lack of basic knowledge; I am still relatively new to this domain.

My question is about the functionality of budget allocation for Purchase Requests (PRs).

Scenario 1:
Let's say we have a situation where we've created a purchase request with 1 line for acquiring 10 items X, with an allocated budget of €10,000. However, we've completed the purchase of all 10 items with an actual cost of €8,000. A PO was made. How can we correctly record the savings of €2,000 in the SAP system and ensure these funds are available for future use in other purchase requests; or is this saving automatically registered when closing the PR in connection to the already existing PO?

Scenario 2:
Now let's say that we bought only 8 pcs of X, so the PO is not related anymore to the full quantity registered in the PR. Should we adjust the quantity first and then close the PR, or it's a useless process and it's ok to just close the PR and the saving will be registered automatically?

Scenario 3:

Lastly, let's consider a scenario where we created a PR with 2 lines: 10 units of X and 10 units of Y. A PO is made for the acquisition of the 10 pcs of X (the first line). The second line is not needed anymore. Should we delete the second line and then close the existing PR so that we can use the saved budget for another new PR?

Thank you in advance for your time! Your help will be truly appreciated!

entry.title: JMS Dead Letter Queue Alert
entry.content:
Dear All,

What is the best way to alert admin when a DLQ queue is filled with some entries? Is there anyway we can move the entries to processing queue without manual intervention?

entry.title: SAP API Management - Expose Integration Flow Endpoint as API
entry.content:
Dear All,

I am working on exposing an Integration Flow Endpoint as an API using API Management. I've followed several blogs and the tutorial "Expose Integration Flow Endpoint as API." However, I am struggling to get my iFlow endpoint to work with certain policies. Could you please advise if you have a good resource or knowledge base that we can follow step-by-step to achieve this?

Here is what I have done so far:

  1. I added SAP CI as an API Provider using the service instance credentials for the "api" plan. I am using OAuth for authentication. The connection test shows as successful.
  2. My iFlow endpoint is something like https://hostname/http/permit/status. The API should handle one permit at a time and update the status using the PUT method.
  3. When I created the API Proxy, I selected the iFlow using the discover option, and all fields were automatically populated, including the Base Path.

My questions are:

  1. The API Base Path was set to /status, and I added v1 in front of it as a version number. However, I believe this should be a unique path across all APIs to avoid conflicts in the future. Can I change this path to something more meaningful, like permitstatus, considering my iFlow endpoint mentioned above? Would this help avoid conflicts with future APIs?
  2. Do I need to add any resources after this? The tutorial talks about using URL as an API provider but doesn't mention my specific case. If I add a PUT resource, what should the path prefix be? Is it "/status"? I tried using /status, but I get an HTTP 404 error when I try out the API.
  3. Additionally, while adding the proxy, the service instance credentials I used are not the same as the ones I used initially when adding CI as a provider. For the OAuth authentication, I supplied the Process Integration service plan "integration flow." When I test this provider, I get a message that indicates there is an issue. Could this discrepancy in credentials be causing problems with the proxy setup?
    • "System is up and reachable. However, the ping check responded with code : 404; Message : Not Found"
    • However, when I initially added CI as a provider and discovered the flows, the connection test was successful. I used the service plan "api" credentials here.
    • "Connection to the system was successful with response code : 200; Message : OK"
  4. I am also not sure how to check logs or traces to understand why the API test is giving a 404 error. Is there any place where I can monitor what has gone wrong? All I can find are the API analytics dashboards, which show call stats but not the reason for failure or logs. Why is there no monitoring similar to Cloud Integration here?

I feel lost amidst multiple blogs and help links, and I haven't been able to find a single place that clearly explains how to expose this endpoint with basic policies like OAuth, Allowed IPs, Exception Handling etc.

Thank you for your help and guidance.

Screenshots:

entry.title: Is there anyway to change default namespace for ABAP
entry.content:
Hi All

Is there anyway to change default namespace for ABAP?

SAP ABAPS are getting created in a default namespace and want to change it .

Also is there anyway to call ABAP function which reside in different namespace using jco library?

entry.title: SAP CI XI Adapter Exception Handling
entry.content:
Dear All,

We have a scenario where we are receiving a REST API call to create a PM notification in CI which will in turn call an ABAP proxy synchrnously. If the call is successful then we receive a notification number as response from the proxy otherwsie an error message will be populated.

REST  <---> CI  <---> ABAP Proxy

Response structure:

 

Successful Response Format:
{
    "NotificationData": {
        "NotificationNo": "235879"
    }
} 

Error Response Format:
{
    "NotificationData": {
        "ErrorMessage": "Notification type is initial"
    }
}

 

In one scenario, I noticed that the ABAP proxy failed as below. But this one was not sent to CI as Error Response format. How do we catch such errors in CI so that the calling application knows what has happened in the backend? I am currently getting it as

An internal server error occured: .
The MPL ID for the failed message is : AGZdKstoGltuiOuyecWCCmDxKhZi

 

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="true"?>

<!-- Call Inbound Proxy -->

-<SAP:Error xmlns:SOAP="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:SAP="http://sap.com/xi/XI/Message/30" SOAP:mustUnderstand="1">

<SAP:Category>XIServer</SAP:Category>

<SAP:Code area="INTERNAL">INCORRECT_PAYLOAD_DATA</SAP:Code>

<SAP:P1/>

<SAP:P2/>

<SAP:P3/>

<SAP:P4/>

<SAP:AdditionalText/>

<SAP:Stack>Error while processing message payload An error occurred when deserializing in the simple transformation program /1SAI/SASAC0FECA0CFC48809F654 Value is longer than the maximum permitted length 40: "CM notification test 02 - malfunction data" </SAP:Stack>

<SAP:Retry>M</SAP:Retry>

</SAP:Error>

 

In CI logs I can see the error message is flowing like below but this one is not going to the sender. I want to send this message to sender. Thanks for the help.

The MPL ID for the failed message is : AGZdKstoGltuiOuyecWCCmDxKhZi

     ErrorDetail         = <detail><SAP:Error xmlns:SAP="http://sap.com/xi/XI/Message/30" xmlns:soap="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"><SAP:Category>XIServer</SAP:Category><SAP:Code area="INTERNAL">INCORRECT_PAYLOAD_DATA</SAP:Code><SAP:P1/><SAP:P2/><SAP:P3/><SAP:P4/><SAP:AdditionalText/><SAP:Stack>Error while processing message payload
An error occurred when deserializing in the simple transformation program /1SAI/SASAC0FECA0CFC48809F654
Value is longer than the maximum permitted length 40: "CM notification test  02 - malfunction data"
</SAP:Stack></SAP:Error></detail>
     ErrorFaultCode      = {http://sap.com/xi/XI/Message/30}XIServer.INTERNAL.INCORRECT_PAYLOAD_DATA

entry.title: Embracing SAP Clean Core: A Strategic Imperative for Digital Transformation
entry.content:
In the rapidly evolving digital landscape, enterprises are constantly seeking ways to streamline operations, enhance agility, and drive innovation. SAP Clean Core emerges as a pivotal strategy in this quest, offering a path to a simplified, efficient, and future-ready enterprise core. This blog post explores the significance of SAP Clean Core, its alignment with SAP Activate methodology, the synergies with RISE with SAP, the transformative capabilities of S/4HANA, and the role of KTern.AI in facilitating this journey.

SAP Activate Methodology: The Blueprint for Success

The SAP Activate methodology is a flexible, agile framework designed to guide businesses through the implementation and deployment of SAP solutions. It encompasses best practices, guided configurations, and a clear methodology to ensure project success. By leveraging SAP Activate, organizations can accelerate their SAP S/4HANA implementations, ensuring a clean core that is devoid of unnecessary customizations and complexity.

RISE with SAP: Elevating Business Transformation

RISE with SAP is a comprehensive offering that simplifies the migration of ERP systems to the cloud. It encapsulates cloud solutions, infrastructure, and services, providing a seamless transition path. With RISE with SAP, businesses can benefit from embedded intelligence, real-time insights, and advanced technologies, all of which contribute to maintaining a clean core and fostering business resilience.

SAP S/4HANA: The Heart of Digital Transformation

At the core of the digital transformation journey lies SAP S/4HANA, SAP’s most advanced ERP suite. Designed to run on the in-memory SAP HANA database, SAP S/4HANA integrates functions from lines of business and industry solutions, offering unparalleled speed, simplicity, and efficiency. A clean core in SAP S/4HANA ensures that businesses can leverage real-time data processing and analytics, essential for informed decision-making and competitive advantage.

 

The Imperative of SAP Clean Core

A clean core is essential for businesses to adapt swiftly to changing market dynamics and customer expectations. It enables organizations to leverage the latest SAP product advancements and integrate new software releases promptly. Without a clean core, businesses face challenges such as increased operational costs, inflexibility, and technical debt, which can hinder innovation and growth.

 

 

Without a clean core, organizations may encounter several challenges:

Technical Challenges

  • Complex Upgrades: Customizations can complicate the upgrade process, leading to longer and more costly system updates
  • Reduced System Performance: A cluttered core with custom code can degrade system performance and reliability
  • Increased Technical Debt: Over time, customizations can accumulate, resulting in technical debt that hampers agility and innovation.
  • Integration Difficulties: Custom code can create dependencies that make it difficult to integrate with other systems or technologies.

Operational Challenges

  • Operational Instability: A non-standard core may lead to unpredictable system behavior and increased downtime
  • Compliance Risks: Deviations from standard configurations can pose risks to regulatory compliance and data integrity
  • Resource Intensiveness: Customized systems require more resources for maintenance and support

Strategic Challenges

  • Inhibiting Digital Transformation: A dirty core can hinder an organization’s ability to adapt to new business models and technologies.
  • Barriers to Innovation: Excessive customizations can limit the ability to leverage new SAP functionalities and innovations.
  • Change Management Difficulties: Transitioning to a clean core requires significant change management efforts, which can be challenging if the existing system is heavily customized.

In summary, not maintaining a clean core can lead to increased costs, reduced efficiency, and a lack of flexibility, which are detrimental to an organization’s digital transformation journey. It’s essential to adopt a strategic approach to ensure a clean core for long-term success and sustainability.

Organizations looking to clean up their SAP core can start by adopting a strategic approach that includes the following steps:

  1. Assessment of Customizations: Begin with a thorough assessment of the existing system to identify all customizations, modifications, and enhancement.
  2. Fit-to-Standard Analysis: Evaluate the necessity of each customization against the standard SAP S/4HANA capabilities. Retire any custom code that is no longer needed or can be replaced by standard functionality.
  3. Leverage SAP Best Practices: Adopt SAP best practices and guided configurations to align with standard processes.
  4. Use of SAP BTP for Extensions: Utilize SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) to build extensions rather than modifying the core system.
  5. API-First Approach: Implement an API-first strategy to facilitate integration without altering the core system.
  6. Continuous Monitoring and Optimization: Establish a process for ongoing monitoring and optimization to ensure the core remains clean and aligned with business needs.
  7. Documentation and Governance: Maintain comprehensive documentation of all changes and establish strong governance to manage technical debt.

 

By following these steps, organizations can systematically clean up their SAP core, leading to a more streamlined, efficient, and agile system that is better equipped for the digital transformation journey.

 Accelerating the Journey to a Clean Core with AI powered Automation

KTern.AI stands out as a digital transformation suite that aids in achieving a clean SAP core It offers digital tools for assessment, governance, process orchestration, testing, and optimization. With KTern.AI, businesses can plan risk-free SAP transformations, ensure productive governance, orchestrate SAP process lifecycles, and avoid disruptions during releases.

In conclusion, maintaining a clean core is not just a technical necessity; it is a strategic imperative that underpins the digital transformation journey. By embracing SAP Activate methodology, RISE with SAP, and SAP S/4HANA, and leveraging tools like KTern.AI, businesses can navigate the complexities of digital transformation with confidence and clarity


For more reading: 

@https://blogs.sap.com/2023/07/01/clean-core-demystified-what-does-it-mean-and-how-to-achieve-it-with... 

@ https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/practicing-clean-core-extensibility-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud... 

.

entry.title: Demystifying DXaaS: Accelerating Digital Transformation with KTern.AI
entry.content:
Demystifying DXaaS: Accelerating Digital Transformation with KTern.AI

In this log post, we’ll delve into the complexities of digital transformation, explore how KTern.AI excels in automating this journey, and showcase a real-world case study that highlights the impact of KTern.AI’s services

In today’s rapidly evolving business landscape, digital transformation is no longer a luxury—it’s a necessity. Organizations across industries are embracing technology to streamline processes, enhance customer experiences, and stay competitive. Enter Digital Transformation as a Service (DXaaS), a powerful approach that combines automation, intelligence, and expertise to drive meaningful change.

The Integration Challenge: A Roadblock to DX Success

 

One of the biggest hurdles in any DX initiative is system integration. Businesses often have a complex IT landscape with disparate systems housing critical data. Integrating these systems to create a unified platform for streamlined operations, data analysis, and automation can be a time-consuming and resource-intensive process. Traditional approaches often involve:

  • High upfront costs: Investing in hardware, software, and specialized IT personnel for integration projects.
  • Lengthy implementation times: Manual configuration and testing of integrations can significantly delay the realization of DX benefits.
  • Increased risk of errors: Complex integrations require meticulous planning and execution, and even minor errors can disrupt operations.

The Energy-Intensive Journey: SAP Integration and Transformation

SAP integration and transformation involve intricate processes, data migrations, and custom code adaptations. These endeavors can be energy-consuming due to several factors:

  1. Legacy Systems: Integrating legacy systems with modern SAP solutions requires meticulous planning and execution. The energy spent on bridging the gap between old and new technologies can be substantial.
  2. Data Complexity: Migrating data from various sources—ERP systems, databases, spreadsheets—demands significant computational resources. Ensuring data integrity and consistency adds to the energy overhead.
  3. Custom Code Modifications: Aligning existing custom code with SAP’s evolving landscape involves careful analysis and adjustments. Each modification consumes time and effort.
  4. Process Mapping: Understanding existing business processes and mapping them to SAP’s best practices can be resource-intensive. Energy is expended in process discovery, documentation, and alignment.
  5. Change Management: Transformations impact people, and managing change requires communication, training, and support. Energy is spent in navigating resistance and ensuring smooth transitions.

Ktern.AI: Automating DX with Expertise and Innovation

This is where Ktern.AI, a leading player in the Kaar Technologies ecosystem, steps in. They leverage their expertise in SAP solutions and a suite of innovative products and

services to automate your DX journey, specifically addressing the integration challenge. Here's how Ktern.AI excels:

  • Pre-built Integration Connectors: Ktern.AI offers pre-configured connectors that seamlessly integrate your existing SAP systems with other applications, eliminating the need for custom coding and saving valuable time.
  • Low-Code Development Platforms: Their low-code development platforms empower businesses to build custom integrations without extensive programming knowledge, democratizing the integration process.
  • Automation Tools and Robotic Process Automation (RPA): Ktern.AI utilizes automation tools and RPA to streamline repetitive integration tasks, minimizing human error and ensuring efficiency.

KTern.AI: Your Energy-Efficient DXaaS Partner

KTern.AI stands at the forefront of DXaaS, offering an AI-driven platform that accelerates SAP digital transformations while minimizing energy consumption. Here’s how KTern.AI excels:

  1. Generative AI: KTern.AI leverages generative AI to build an intelligent enterprise. From assessing SAP processes to creating roadmaps, it empowers organizations with data-driven insights, reducing manual effort.
  2. No-Code Automation: With KTern.AI, you don’t need to be a developer. Its no-code approach allows business users to automate processes, saving energy by eliminating complex coding tasks.
  3. Clean Core Strategy: KTern.AI follows SAP’s Clean Core Strategy, minimizing custom code adaptations during upgrades. This approach reduces energy spent on code maintenance.
  4. Comprehensive Streams: KTern.AI covers five critical automation streams:
  • Digital Maps: Assess SAP processes, identify energy-intensive areas, and create transformation roadmaps
  • Digital Projects: Govern SAP projects efficiently, optimizing energy usage through streamlined collaboration and signoff workflows
  • Digital Process: Orchestrate SAP processes with minimal custom code adaptations, conserving energy while ensuring efficiency
  • Digital Labs: Optimize testing, reduce energy-intensive test cycles, and enhance reliability.
  • Digital Mines: Avoid business disruptions during releases, optimizing energy usage in SAP DevOps
 
 

 

 

Case Study: How Ktern.AI Helped a Manufacturing Giant Streamline Operations

A leading manufacturing company faced significant challenges due to disconnected systems and manual data entry processes. Ktern.AI implemented a DXaaS solution that included:

  • Integrating their SAP ERP system with production machinery and warehouse management systems.
  • Utilizing pre-built connectors and low-code development platforms to automate data flow.
  • Deploying RPA bots to handle repetitive tasks like order processing and inventory updates.

A Sustainable Transformation

Let’s explore a real-world case study. A global enterprise faced an energy challenge during its SAP S/4HANA migration. KTern.AI stepped in:

  1. Assessment: KTern.AI conducted a rapid assessment, identifying energy-intensive processes and areas.
  2. Governance: Using the Activate cockpit, the company achieved productive governance, minimizing energy waste.
  3. Orchestration: KTern.AI orchestrated processes, adapting custom code judiciously to conserve energy.
  4. Testing Excellence: The Fiori test auto-captures and defect management ensured efficient testing without unnecessary energy expenditure.
  5. Risk-Free Release: The enterprise achieved a risk-free SAP release, saving energy and maintaining business continuity

Results:

  • Reduced integration time by 60% compared to traditional methods.
  • Eliminated manual data entry errors leading to improved data accuracy.
  • Achieved a 20% increase in operational efficiency.

Embrace a Seamless DX Journey with Ktern.AI

Don't let integration complexities hinder your digital transformation journey. Leverage the power of DXaaS and Ktern.AI's automation expertise to streamline your integrations, unlock data silos, and achieve operational excellence.

Ready to take the first step? Contact Ktern.AI today and let them guide you towards a successful and automated digital transformation.

By embracing DXaaS and partnering with Ktern.AI, you can unlock the full potential of your SAP landscape and propel your business towards a thriving future.

entry.title: Complex data export
entry.content:
Hi!

Currently, we need to export data for all company composite roles along with information on what level of access they provide (editing/viewing/etc.). 

So, my question is:
How can I export data for all composite roles in my company? The data must contain information such as:
Composite role - each single role included in the composite role - transactions assigned to a single role - access level of each transaction included in a given single role.

Could someone tell me what transactions/tables/data should be used to export such data?

If I have posted a post, please bear with me and move it to the appropriate section.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: How to change storage bin and type in outbound delivery ?
entry.content:
Dear Friends ,

I'm using 
1 ) BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_CHANGE : for update quantity , For existing record .
2 ) L_TO_CREATE_DN  : for create TO for outbound delivery .
3 ) L_TO_CONFIRM  : for confirm TO generated by Bapi "L_TO_CREATE_DN" .
4 ) WS_DELIVERY_UPDATE : for creating PGI .

Is there any way  BAPI to change the Storage bin and type for batch item in outbound delivery .

Please help me on this .

Regards ,
Vasanth

entry.title: Use customer hierarchy level 3 as sold-to for output conditions
entry.content:
Hello Masters!

When configuring the output conditions for ORDRSP or INVOIC ( VV11/VV31) which are base on the sold-to/Bill-to, it is common that there will be quite a lot of sold-to/bill-to numbers to put in, especially if one has to do it for each output type. I am wondering if a higher customer number, such as customer hierarchy level 3 or level 4 could be used instead as the sold-to/s are in lower level (depending on how you see it) level 6.

entry.title: Integration between Recruiting and a Corporate Website - Job Postings
entry.content:
Hi Team.

I am trying to build an integration between SF Recruiting & a corporate website. I can find all of the data points I need, but I don't know on which data points to filter to only receive the active job postings on the external career website.

Does anyone have the answer to this?

entry.title: Automatic replication of custom table data upon update
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a requirement in which there is a custom table with customer/vendor as key fields and other custom fields in which this will be updated from and RFC call from external system and once data is updated in the table the specific customer/vendor data has to be replicated to target systems. 

Can you guys please help me how this can be achieved.

entry.title: ERROR => IcmConnInitServerSSL: SapSSLSessionStartNB returned (-127) dev_webdisp
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

After updating a certificate in the PSE list, the Webdispatcher was restarted this startup but the ports were not listening

Checking logs we found the error:

ERROR: SapSSLSessionStartNB(sssl_hdl=7efc5400a5e0)==SSSLERR_ALERT_CERTIFICATE_UNKNOWN
ERROR => IcmConnInitServerSSL: SapSSLSessionStartNB returned (-127): SSSLERR_ALERT_CERTIFICATE_UNKNOWN

 

 

by comparing the files SAPSSLS.pse we see that they have different permissions

the one above in the image is the new one and the one below is the old one that was backed up for security.

Could this be the mistake?

thanks in advance 

Regards,

Nelson

entry.title: Agregar Campos en el formulario Cuentas Bancaria propia & SN
entry.content:
Consulta 1

la consulta es, como se puede visualizar en esta pantalla Cuentas Bancarias propia; la visibilidad del campo Currency (Moneda) debido al momento de consultar la tabla DSC1 si existe el campo, nos ayuda se puede  donde que sea visible este campo aparezca en SAP.

 

En la segunda consulta, si se puede agregar un campo llamado Currency (Moneda)  en este formulario en Cuentas bancarias de Socio de Negocios , debido que accedí a UDF no encuentro para adicionar un campo en esa tabla, y verifico que la tabla se llama OCRB.

Como puedo agregar campos a eso dos formulario ?

entry.title: connect google drive with S4/HANA version 2020
entry.content:
Hi 

Exist the guide for configure the integration with Google drive in S4/HANA 2020?

Please share with me

Thanks

entry.title: BRIM Integration/ Strategical relevance of BTP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi folks,

Today I got into trouble with a customer who we have sold Integration Suite as THE strategic platform for his enterprise integration needs. The customer already uses DI for data integration and is super SAP heavy/centric. Now one of the first new projects is introducing SAP BRIM. And I had to learn that SAP proposed to our customer not to use integration suite but instead to use convergent mediation (https://www.sap.com/germany/products/financial-management/convergent-mediation.html) which I did not even know about before.

My customer is furious that we proposed integration suite and now they need another integration product in their first project. Also I saw some BRIM standard content on the business hub, but at the same time convergent mediation seems to be the tool of choice. Is there anybody out there who already implemented BRIM and had to decide between the two integration solutions? Is IS really not suited equally?

This case also makes me wonder why sap would not go for an integration suite first approach for such cases. With the ERP getting more and more modular I am afraid that we will get more such cases where micro solutions get their own integration solution bundled. That would make IS with its recent price raise a more difficult sell since the strategic importance could be questioned.

 Would be interesting to get the communities take on this and some kind of guidance would be highly appreciated.

Thanks,

Peter

 

SAP Integration Strategy SAP Integration Suite @UdoPaltzer @svenhuberti

entry.title: Error in Scrap Sales Order
entry.content:
While creating scrap sale order below error occurred. Please do the needful.
"Material 8000000011 is not defined for sales org. D001, distr.chan.40, language EN" 
"Sales from sales organization, D001 dist. chan.40, plant D101 are not defined"

entry.title: Quote comparison in sap
entry.content: Quote Comparison in SAP.

entry.title: New tanks were created for plant 3001, when testing Dips in O4_TIGER it posts but in zmm_silo_stock
entry.content: New tanks were created for plant 3001, when testing Dips in O4_TIGER it posts but in zmm_silo_stock we get the below error.
